message:
feat(plaid,qdrant,slack): provisioning automation with doctor diagnostics and operation receipts

Implement the provisioning automation pattern for Plaid, Qdrant, and
Slack connectors — the same multi-auth, self-check, and doctor
infrastructure previously added for Microsoft 365, Telegram, and
Anthropic connectors.

Plaid connector — full provisioning automation:
- PlaidAuthMode enum: Secret(String) vs CredentialId for egress proxy
- PlaidConfig struct with client_id, auth, environment, base_url
- PlaidEnvironment enum (Sandbox/Development/Production) with URL
  mapping and host validation
- handle_doctor() with 6 checks: configuration, auth_mode,
  network_constraints, environment_selection, credential_materialization,
  credentials_valid (live link_token_create probe)
- URL normalization and localhost detection helpers

Qdrant connector — provisioning automation + 4 new operations:
- QdrantAuthMode enum: ApiKey vs CredentialId with URL validation
- handle_doctor() with 5 checks including live list_collections probe
- New operations: create_collection, delete_collection (dangerous),
  query_points, batch_query_points
- OperationReceipt on all write operations (upsert, delete, create,
  delete collection) for audit trail
- New client methods (create_collection, delete_collection, query_points,
  batch_query_points, put_json, delete) with comprehensive unit tests
- Updated manifest with 4 new operation definitions and interface hash

Slack connector — doctor diagnostics + operation receipts:
- auth_test() client method calling Slack auth.test with scope extraction
- handle_doctor() with 3 checks: token_present, token_valid, scopes_valid
- OperationReceipt on all side-effecting operations (post_message,
  reply_thread, upload_file, add_reaction, set_channel_topic)
- Refined error mapping: missing_scope, not_in_channel, restricted_action,
  ekm_access_denied, access_denied now map to CapabilityDenied
- New types: DoctorReport, DoctorCheck, AuthTestInfo, OperationReceipt
- Integration tests for doctor (4 scenarios) and receipt emission (5 ops)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@ min_mesh_version = "2.0.0"
 min_protocol = "fcp2-sym/2.0"

+interface_hash = "blake3-256:fcp.interface.v2:a0d87d49fd595446563a5a7af3c03f23fa5dd3fe8f63b1a1a8bea272aba14c71"
 
 [connector]
 id = "fcp.qdrant"
@@ -50,6 +50,66 @@ forbidden = [
     "media.download",
 ]
 
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points"]
+description = "Execute multiple vector queries in one request"
+capability = "qdrant.points.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "strict"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".input_schema]
+required = [
+    "collection_name",
+    "queries",
+]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".input_schema.properties.collection_name]
+type = "string"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".input_schema.properties.queries]
+description = "Array of query request objects"
+type = "array"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".output_schema]
+required = ["result"]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".output_schema.properties.result]
+type = "array"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".network_constraints]
+host_allow = ["*.cloud.qdrant.io"]
+port_allow = [
+    443,
+    6333,
+    6334,
+]
+ip_allow = []
+cidr_deny = []
+deny_localhost = true
+deny_private_ranges = true
+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+spki_pins = []
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 3
+connect_timeout_ms = 10000
+total_timeout_ms = 30000
+max_response_bytes = 10485760
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.batch_query_points".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Batch independent vector queries to reduce request overhead."
+common_mistakes = ["Submitting oversized query batches that exceed service limits."]
+examples = ['{"collection_name": "docs", "queries": [{"query": [0.1, 0.2, 0.3], "limit": 5}]}']
+related = [
+    "qdrant.query_points",
+    "qdrant.search",
+]
+
 [provides.operations."qdrant.collection_info"]
 description = "Get collection configuration and statistics"
 capability = "qdrant.collections.read"
@@ -157,6 +217,155 @@ common_mistakes = []
 examples = ['{"collection_name": "docs", "exact": true}']
 related = ["qdrant.scroll"]
 
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection"]

+idempotency = "none"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".input_schema]
+required = [
+    "collection_name",
+    "vectors",
+]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".input_schema.properties.collection_name]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".input_schema.properties.hnsw_config]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".input_schema.properties.wal_config]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".input_schema.properties.write_consistency_factor]

+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".output_schema]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".output_schema.properties.status]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.create_collection".network_constraints]

+[provides.operations."qdrant.delete_collection"]

 [provides.operations."qdrant.delete_points"]

+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points"]
+description = "Query points using Qdrant query API"
+capability = "qdrant.points.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "strict"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema]
+required = [
+    "collection_name",
+    "query",
+]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.collection_name]
+type = "string"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.filter]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.limit]
+type = "integer"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.query]
+description = "Query payload (vector, id, or hybrid query object)"
+type = "array"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.score_threshold]
+type = "number"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.with_payload]
+default = true
+type = "boolean"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".input_schema.properties.with_vectors]
+default = false
+type = "boolean"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".output_schema]
+required = ["result"]
+type = "object"
+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".output_schema.properties.result]

+
+[provides.operations."qdrant.query_points".network_constraints]

+port_allow = [
+    443,
+    6333,
+    6334,
+]
+ip_allow = []
+cidr_deny = []
+deny_localhost = true
+deny_private_ranges = true
+deny_tailnet_ranges = true
+require_sni = true
+spki_pins = []
+deny_ip_literals = true
+require_host_canonicalization = true
+dns_max_ips = 16
+max_redirects = 3

@@ -557,6 +855,13 @@ window_ms = 60000
 burst = 10

+id = "qdrant.collections.write"
+requests = 30
+window_ms = 60000
+burst = 5
+scope = "instance"
+
 [[rate_limits.pools]]
 id = "qdrant.points.read"
 requests = 300
@@ -572,11 +877,15 @@ burst = 20

-"qdrant.upsert_points" = ["qdrant.points.write"]
+"qdrant.query_points" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
+"qdrant.scroll" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
+"qdrant.batch_query_points" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
 "qdrant.search" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
-"qdrant.delete_points" = ["qdrant.points.write"]
-"qdrant.collection_info" = ["qdrant.collections.read"]
+"qdrant.upsert_points" = ["qdrant.points.write"]
+"qdrant.delete_collection" = ["qdrant.collections.write"]
 "qdrant.list_collections" = ["qdrant.collections.read"]
-"qdrant.scroll" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
 "qdrant.get_points" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
 "qdrant.count" = ["qdrant.points.read"]
+"qdrant.delete_points" = ["qdrant.points.write"]
+"qdrant.collection_info" = ["qdrant.collections.read"]
+"qdrant.create_collection" = ["qdrant.collections.write"]

@@ -97,6 +97,7 @@ async fn handle_message(connector: &mut SlackConnector, message: &str) -> serde_
         "configure" => connector.handle_configure(params).await,
         "handshake" => connector.handle_handshake(params).await,
         "health" => connector.handle_health().await,
+        "doctor" => connector.handle_doctor().await,
         "introspect" => connector.handle_introspect().await,
         "invoke" => connector.handle_invoke(params).await,
         "simulate" => connector.handle_simulate(params).await,

@@ -192,3 +192,55 @@ pub struct ResponseMetadata {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub next_cursor: Option<String>,
 }
+
+/// Result of Slack `auth.test` — identifies the token holder.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct AuthTestInfo {
+    pub url: String,
+    pub team: String,
+    pub user: String,
+    pub team_id: String,
+    pub user_id: String,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub bot_id: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub is_enterprise_install: bool,
+}
+
+/// auth.test response wrapper.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
+pub struct AuthTestData {
+    pub url: String,
+    pub team: String,
+    pub user: String,
+    pub team_id: String,
+    pub user_id: String,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub bot_id: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub is_enterprise_install: bool,
+}
+
+/// Audit receipt for side-effecting operations.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
+pub struct OperationReceipt {
+    pub operation: String,
+    pub effect: String,
+    pub resource: String,
+    pub timestamp: String,
+}
+
+/// Provisioning doctor report for readiness validation.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
+pub struct DoctorReport {
+    pub ready: bool,
+    pub checks: Vec<DoctorCheck>,
+}
+
+/// A single doctor check result.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
+pub struct DoctorCheck {
+    pub name: String,
+    pub passed: bool,
+    pub message: String,
+}
